title: Weather Today [02 April, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Snow Alert, Thunderstorms & Heat Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad, Uttar Pradesh - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
Disclaimer: Weather forecasts are for guidance only. Conditions may change rapidly. Always check local authorities for emergencies and travel alerts.
India’s weather today (April 2, 2026) shows a mixed pattern with rainfall, thunderstorms, and rising temperatures across several regions. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), western disturbances and seasonal summer patterns are shaping weather conditions across the country, bringing rain to parts of North India while heat intensifies in southern and eastern regions.
India’s weather today (April 2, 2026) shows a mixed pattern with rainfall, thunderstorms, and rising temperatures across several regions. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), western disturbances and seasonal summer patterns are shaping weather conditions across the country, bringing rain to parts of North India while heat intensifies in southern and eastern regions.
Below is a detailed city-wise and region-wise forecast for today.
Weather systems moving from the Mediterranean toward the Himalayas are likely to trigger rainfall and snowfall across parts of the Western Himalayan region. Areas in Jammu and Kashmir and nearby mountainous regions may experience scattered rain and snowfall due to an active western disturbance.
Meteorologists say these systems bring moisture and cloud cover, which can produce snowfall at higher elevations while plains receive rain and thunderstorms.
Northern plains including Delhi, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan may witness intermittent rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds. These conditions are expected to temporarily lower daytime temperatures and delay the onset of intense heatwaves in April.
In Delhi NCR, the weather is expected to remain partly cloudy with chances of light showers and gusty winds. Temperatures may hover around 23°C–34°C, while upcoming western disturbances may bring thunderstorms over the next few days.
Chennai is likely to experience hot and humid weather today with rising temperatures. IMD has warned that the region could see multiple heatwave days during the summer season, although occasional showers may bring temporary relief.
Weather in Mumbai will remain mostly warm and humid with partly cloudy skies. While the city itself may remain dry today, parts of Maharashtra could experience thunderstorms and light rain in the coming days.
Hyderabad is expected to see warm daytime temperatures along with dry weather conditions. Telangana may witness a higher number of heatwave days during the April–June period compared to normal years.
Across Karnataka, interior districts may see isolated thunderstorms due to pre-monsoon activity. Bengaluru and surrounding areas could experience partly cloudy skies with moderate temperatures.
Weather across Uttar Pradesh is expected to remain partly cloudy with chances of scattered rainfall and thunderstorms in some districts due to western disturbances.
Several areas in Rajasthan may experience cloud cover, gusty winds, and isolated rain showers over the next couple of days. The weather disturbance could also lead to temporary cooling across the region.
Higher elevations in Jammu and Kashmir may receive snowfall or rain as western disturbances remain active in the region.
Weather in Ahmedabad is expected to stay warm with partly cloudy skies. Recent observations show a slight dip in maximum temperatures due to cloud cover, with temperatures around 36–37°C. Light rain or thundershowers may affect parts of Gujarat from April 3 onward.
Punjab may witness scattered rainfall and thunderstorms in some areas as changing atmospheric conditions influence North India’s weather pattern.
Across India today:
Despite rain in some regions, many parts of central, western, and southern India are experiencing rising temperatures during afternoon hours. Heat discomfort may increase, especially between 12 PM and 4 PM, prompting health advisories such as staying hydrated and limiting sun exposure.
Air quality in Delhi is expected to remain in the Moderate category, with AQI levels hovering around 90–100 thanks to recent rainfall and improved wind movement.
Thunderstorms and gusty winds may affect parts of North India. Travelers should monitor local advisories and avoid outdoor activity during lightning or storms.
